name: Potential Web Shell Dropped
notes:
This rule detects potential web shells dropped

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Florian Roth (rule), MSTI (query, idea)
SIGMA Title: Suspicious ASPX File Drop by Exchange
SIGMA Description: Detects suspicious file type dropped by an Exchange component in IIS into a suspicious folder


Confidence: MEDIUM

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.File Created
when the event matches LOWER("Target File Directory") MATCHES '.*(frontend\\httpproxy\\|\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\aspnet_client\\).*' AQL filter query
when the event matches LOWER("File Extension") MATCHES '(aspx|asp|ashx|ps1|bat|exe|dll|vbs)' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Potential Web Shell Dropped on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.File Created
AND when the event matches LOWER("Target File Directory") MATCHES '.*(frontend\\httpproxy\\|\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\aspnet_client\\).*' AQL filter query
AND when the event matches LOWER("File Extension") MATCHES '(aspx|asp|ashx|ps1|bat|exe|dll|vbs)' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Potential Web Shell Dropped
Event description: Detects potential web shells dropped
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
